Visual Constitution · Working Draft · July 2026

Hegemony

Brandbook · v0.1

The board is the clay; the interface is the glaze. One ceramic language — quiet where the game is played, ceremonial where the game speaks.

The game is light-mode — parchment, always. This document has a dark reading theme for comfort, but flip it and watch the game-chrome specimens below: they stay parchment, exactly as the game will. In-game, black glaze exists only as the ceremony register — the vase-black verb rail, tooltips, and the game-over tableau. Headings are set in Cinzel, text in Alegreya — the two self-hosted faces this book ratifies.

I

Ground Truth

Before prescribing, the evidence. Six audit agents read every component, style sheet, asset, and design doc; the live app was screenshotted at 1440×900 and 1280×800. The diagnosis confirms all three of your complaints — and finds more to love than expected.

  1. Box-on-box chrome Every element carries its own border and background — panel frames with ornate red corner marks, chip borders inside card borders inside panel borders. Hierarchy is drawn, not composed.
  2. Three icon languages at once Flat tinted silhouettes (resource masks), engraved terracotta line art (atlases), and modern self-colored vectors (green/red theatre masks) ship simultaneously — sometimes for the same concept. Phase 1 concepts (bank, calm, ladder, ventures, riot, event tables) have no icons at all.
  3. Three modal grammars Ornate event card / plain cream card / unthemed white dialog — because the modal scaffold is copy-pasted nine times with no shared component.
  4. Placeholder board tokens White square pips and circled dots read as debug output; settlement markers are flat primary-color squares and triangles in off-palette Tailwind hues (#1e3a8a #eab308 #7c3aed #c1121f).
  5. Type anarchy Two good font stacks — then 36 distinct font sizes, ~50 literal combinations, and one micro-label style re-implemented twelve times with twelve different numbers. Copperplate is macOS-only; Windows silently becomes a different game.
  6. Palette drift Ten near-duplicate parchment tans, seven near-blacks, and ~200 alpha-literal colors that bypass the token layer — a reskin today would only restyle about 40% of the UI.

What is already excellent — the brandbook builds on these, not over them

Also keep: the painterly Aegean sea chart (the board's mat and the game's one deliberate contrast), the semantic token layer in base.css (well-designed, merely bypassed), the mask-tint icon pipeline, and zero !important in 4,502 lines of CSS.

II

The Five Laws

Constraints promoted to constitution. Every future screen, asset, and component is tested against these — they encode decisions you already made, sometimes the hard way.

Α

The tile is a stage, not a picture

Buildings, colonies, and pops sit on hexes — unlike Catan's intersections, your terrain is load-bearing. So the ground is a flat glaze fill (Law Β gives it its hue) and nothing more: no illustration, no texture, no gradient, no art. Everything drawn on a tile is a game piece or printed data, and pieces must always out-read the ground.

Because you already tried vase-relief tiles and rolled them back for readability — the regret was art on the play surface, and that ban is law (docs/resource-color-rollback-note.md).
Β

Color means kind on the ground, owner on the pieces

Terrain fill hue = tile kind — four flat fired glazes (wheat plains, olive forest, sienna hill, grey mountain), no gradients, no illustration. Owner glaze appears only on tokens and territory rims, and never touches terrain without its keylines (ink outside, ivory inside), so a rim reads on any fill including its own hue. Status colors appear only beside a sign or glyph, never alone. (Amended 2026-07-13: v0.1 neutralized terrain to a ceramic ramp with kind as a faint emblem — the owner correctly ruled that a regression: hue is the one preattentive channel, and kind is the most-scanned fact on the board.)

Because the rollback you did was about illustration on tiles, not color — art was the problem, and a 30%-ink emblem cannot carry what a fill carries for free.
Γ

One panel treatment, zero nested boxes

Hierarchy is typography, hairlines, and air. A chrome region gets at most one surface treatment; rows separate by rule, groups by spacing, emphasis by type role. Borders-within-borders are forbidden.

Because the flat-AAA look you want is not a style applied to boxes — it is the deletion of the boxes.
Δ

Ceremony only at moments

Ornament — engraved frames, parchment texture, card art, the meander — concentrates entirely in moments: event reveals, dice tables, riots, game over. Persistent chrome stays silent so the moments land loud.

Because ceremony everywhere is ceremony nowhere; the event cards feel special today precisely because the chrome around them shouldn't compete.
Ε

Laptop first, map first, numbers are data

Designed for 1280×800 through 1600×900, no page scroll, mouse-first (hover assists, never gatekeeps). The map must gain width in every redesign. Costs and rates are ruleset tunables — never baked into art or fixed copy.

Because this is a strategy game played on a laptop in a browser, and every balance number in the spec is expected to move.
III

Palette

Kerameikos, ratified. Every value below either is an existing token or lands on one — this is a collapse and a promotion, not a new palette. Ten tans become three; seven near-blacks become three; the accent family stays exactly what it is.

Inks & grounds — 3 + 3, replacing 7 + 10

#181210
Glaze
Fired-black chrome ground, token linework, tooltips
= --vase-black
#241915
Glaze-Raised
Hover rows, raised dark surfaces
= --ink-2 (revived)
#241910
Ink
All text and lines on light surfaces; greys only as alpha steps of this
= --fg
#FFF8E7
Ivory
Ceremony plates, cards, brightest text on glaze
= --ivory
#F4E6C8
Bone
Paper ground, body text on dark, icon tint on glaze
= --bone
#D8C8A8
Ceramic
Hex terrain ground — the only tan allowed on the board
= --terrain-neutral

Deleted: --panel/--panel-strong/--panel-soft translucencies, the six drifting card-gradient tans, #0b0705, #27150d, and every literal rgb(255 248 231 / x%) — alpha variants derive from these six via color-mix() or RGB-triplet tokens.

Chroma & status — unchanged hexes, ratified roles

#C0461C
Clay
The one interactive accent: primary fill, active states, selection
= --clay / --accent
#8F2E13
Clay-Deep
Pressed states; negative status (losses, deficits, riot)
= --clay-deep
#D98A35
Ochre
Warnings, costs, attention; gold resource in icon contexts
= --ochre
#5E6E3A
Olive
Positive status: gains, surplus, growth
= --olive
#1F6977
Aegean
Navigation & information: legal targets, focus rings, links. Never a fill.
= --aegean
#722420
Oxblood
Ceremonial danger only: riot reveal, defeat
= --oxblood (revived)

Player glazes — six seats, replacing the Tailwind primaries

Pigments a ceramicist owned, spread on a lightness ladder so seats survive color-blind hue collapse. Structural safety net: every owner token carries an ivory keyline plus an engraved Greek ordinal blazon (Α Β Γ Δ Ε Ζ) — color is never the only channel. Four-player games use seats 1–4, which are pairwise safe under all three dichromacies.

Α
Kyanos
#1F6977 · L*40
Β
Miltos
#9C2F1B · L*34
Γ
Melichron
#D98A35 · L*64
Δ
Chloros
#55673A · L*41
Ε
Porphyra
#6E3A5C · L*33
Ζ
Glaukos
#3B3E48 · L*27

Miltos is deliberately darker and browner than Clay so player-red and action-red never confuse. Melichron shares Ochre's hue — acceptable because resource hues appear only in pills/icons and owner glaze only on tokens/rims (Law Β keeps them apart) — but as the lightest seat it carries a mandatory 2px ink contact line and needs a real-board contrast check. A vase-white "Leukos" seat was proposed and struck by the judge panel: white glaze on bone plains tiles is the vase-relief mistake reborn — ownership would vanish for one of four default players. Glaukos (cool slate, distinct from the warm inks) takes seat Ζ instead.

IV

Typography

The current instinct — inscriptional capitals over an old-style serif — is correct and stays. What changes: the faces become self-hosted webfonts so Windows players see the same game, and 36 ad-hoc sizes become eight ratified roles. You are reading both faces right now.

RoleFaceLicensePurpose
DisplayCinzel 600/700 — Trajanic inscriptional capitalsSIL OFL, self-host woff2 ≈26KB/wtThe stone-cut voice: titles, verbs, labels, numerals-as-monument. Rescues the Copperplate intent from macOS-only. Fallback: Copperplate → Georgia.
BodyAlegreya 400/500/700 + italic — old-style literary serifSIL OFL, ≈43KB/wtCard text, chronicle, tooltips; real italics for flavor lines; true tnum/lnum tabular figures for every game number. Fallback: Iowan Old Style → Palatino → Georgia.

The eight roles — sizes @1440; one rem step (×0.93) covers 1280

displayCinzel 700 · 28px · +0.06em · caps
Bread & Circuses
panel-titleCinzel 700 · 15px · +0.08em · caps
The Ledger of the Polis
verbCinzel 700 · 13px · +0.10em · caps
Found · Upgrade · Calm · Venture
labelCinzel 600 · 11px · +0.14em · caps
Settlement Target — replaces all 12 hand-rolled micro-labels
bodyAlegreya 400 · 14.5px · lh 1.45
Add one citizen to an owned settlement with available capacity. The bank never barters; every round trip pays the spread.
body-emAlegreya 400 italic
Amphorae for the Tyrrhenian markets — if the sea allows.
statAlegreya 700 · 15px · tnum lnum
24 · 18 (+8) · 12 (+4) · −0.5
stat-largeAlegreya 700 · 20px · tnum lnum
Year 1 · 28 seasons remain · d6 → 5

Hard rules: Cinzel never below 10px and never renders in-game quantities (its figures are monumental, not tabular). Every quantity — pills, ledger, costs, dice — takes a .num utility (font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums lining-nums) so columns align like a real ledger. Deltas always signed, with a true minus (−), colored by status. CI greps for font-size: outside the role sheet.

V

Iconography

Ratified by experiment (2026-07-13): the stylized direction. Two antagonistic arms were built on identical bases and judged on blind glance-tasks — dedicated Greek pictograms vs. zero icons. Stylized won where it matters most (terrain scanning on the board — the plain arm's hue-only chits failed the twin-tans and colorblind reads) and tied everywhere else, provided every glyph obeys the legibility law below. The owner has ruled for stylized. One language: solid Greek pictograms — silhouette-first (Style C's weight) drawing Style A's subjects — cut once, rendered two ways: currentColor SVG for chrome, terracotta duotone of the same drawing for ceremony surfaces.

The legibility law — the five rules the winning arm was built under, now binding

  • Silhouette-first: every glyph must be identifiable as a solid one-ink mass at its render size (12–20px); interior detail (knockouts, flutes, eyes) only above 20px. The A/B run's casualties are precedent: the drachma loses its owl below 16px, the theatre mask keeps only its mouth, the trireme drops its painted eye.
  • Minimum 2px features at render size; no fills lighter than 60% ink; 12px hard floor — where a station can't afford 12px, the icon is dropped for text, not shrunk.
  • Icons never replace words on actions: verbs, buttons, and warnings keep their labels; pictograms accompany. Never icon-only without an adjacent numeral or word, except settlement kind (which sits inside an owner ring).
  • One glyph = one resource, everywhere — the chit's wheat is the spine's wheat is the ledger's column header. The judge's field-vs-list doctrine decides placement: icons are mandatory where a field of small like objects is scanned (yield chits, settlement kinds, spine, ledger delta headers); text owns every surface read as a list (verb costs, pop chips, chronicle, tooltips, tabs).
  • Format split (proven by the test builds): all UI glyphs ≤32px are hand-authored inline SVG tinted via currentColor — crisper than raster masks at small sizes and free to re-cut. Nano Banana raster is reserved for ceremony art: event cards, the season medallion painting, the game-over tableau, compendium frontispiece. The winning arm's ~30 SVG pictograms are the starter set.

The glyph inventory — now an SVG cut-list (UI, ~30 glyphs, starter set exists in the stylized showcase) plus a small Banana ceremony batch (cards · medallion · tableau · frontispiece)

SetGlyphsNotes
Resources ×6wood · stone · gold (drachma owl) · food · influence · unrestRe-cut in the one language; drop-in mask replacements
Happiness ×2comedy / tragedy masksEngraved pair replaces the modern colored vectors; magnitude shown by the numeral beside them
Pops ×3citizen (laureled profile) · freeman (bare profile) · slave (bound profile)Vase-figure profiles
Phase 1 gaps ×9bank/trapeza · civic calm (kylix + olive sprig) · social ladder · Merchant Convoy (trireme) · Grand Embassy (tripod) · Colonists' Voyage (charter scroll) · riot (toppled krater) · d6 · d20Currently icon-less concepts get first-class glyphs
Verbs ×7 + systemgrow · move · found · upgrade · build · end-turn hourglass · compendium scroll · chronicle quillVerb bar + reference surfaces
Buildings ×4 + seasons ×1marketplace · temple · workshop · granary re-cut; winter re-drawnWinter currently breaks the season set (metallic 3D snowflake)
Forward slots ×3Assembly urn (Ph. 3) · port/luxury amphora (Ph. 4) · landmark laurels (Ph. 2)Draw while the kiln is hot

Housekeeping in the same pass: split atlases into individual transparent sprites (cells currently carry baked parchment squares), fix the unrest-mask/happiness miswiring in map.css:218, delete ~4.3MB of unused source intermediates and checkerboard settlement PNGs, and pick one card-border treatment (meander) for the event-card set.

VI

The Board

The one place all five laws meet. Terrain wears four flat kind-glazes — hue reads at a glance; the bone yield chit confirms kind and carries the number; tokens are lacquered pieces with owner glaze, keylines, and ordinal blazon. Below — the token grammar rendered live (SVG, using the exact values of this book). Note the board stays clay-light in both page themes: the board is constant; only the glaze around it changes.

How the tile speaks, in order of distance. From across the room, hue names the kind — that is what the four glazes are for. A step closer, the bone yield chit (a fired-clay token in the Catan-chit lineage: ink emblem + tabular numeral on a bone plate) confirms the kind for colorblind players and carries the number — its contrast is independent of the fill, which is what the v0.1 engraved-into-clay treatment got wrong. Slot sockets are printed on every tile, settled or not (owner amendment: slots are strategy data you read when choosing where to found). Pops always render as class glyph + count numeral — never one mark per pop; ten citizens is "●×10", not ten circles. Only the capacity chip remains interaction-surfaced.

ElementSpecReplaces
Terrain groundFour flat kind-glazes, fired into the Kerameikos family: plains wheat #D8BE73 · forest olive #75824A · hill sienna #C08243 · mountain grey #A8A290. No gradients, no texture, no art. (Owner ruling 2026-07-13 — supersedes v0.1's neutral ramp, which buried tile kind in a faint emblem.)Both the Catan-primary checkerboard and v0.1's over-quiet neutral ramp
Tile kind + yieldThe bone yield chit: ink terrain emblem + tabular yield numeral stamped on a small bone plate, upper-center, always on — contrast independent of the fill; the emblem doubles as colorblind redundancy and scales to Phase 2's acropolis/landmark kinds where hue alone runs outThe engraved-into-clay treatment (failed contrast) and the white yield plates
SettlementOwner-glaze disc (Metropolis 38 / City 32 / Colony 26px), ink contact line + ivory keyline, existing kind-glyph masks in bone, 9px ordinal blazon at basePrimary-color squares & triangles
PopsClass glyph + count numeral per class present (citizen dotted disc ×2, freeman ring ×1, slave bar ×2) — always numerals at any count, never one mark per pop (owner amendment)Circled-dot pips
Building slotsSockets on every tile at rest, settled or not — count = the tile's real slot total (a metropolis shows its +2): built = bone tessera with the building's engraved glyph, empty = 12% ink recess (owner amendment: slots are printed data like yields)White square pips
Pop capacityDark chip beside the disc, housed/capacity in tabular numerals; appears on selection & Near zoomNothing legible on-map
OwnershipFull-opacity glaze rim sandwiched by 1px ink (outer) and 1px ivory (inner) keylines — never a tile tint. The keylines are what let an olive rim survive an olive forest and a honey rim a sienna hill: no glaze touches terrain without its keylinesThe 22–55% rim whispers of earlier drafts
Zoom LODsFar: hue + chit + disc + rim + ×N · Mid: + pop glyphs, all sockets · Near: + capacity chip, settlement nameOne density at all zooms
VII

Component Grammar

Live specimens, rendered by this page's stylesheet from the book's own tokens — and pinned to the game's chrome, which is parchment-light in both reading themes. The single dark band below is the game's one deliberate exception.

Panel buttons — on the paper chrome (hover them)
The verb rail — the one vase-black band on the parchment shell (verdict graft, §X)

One filled button per screen (the primary — END TURN lives on the rail as a clay seal). Verbs are text + icon with a sliding clay underline — the affordance is the underline, not a box. Against the dark rail, enabled-vs-disabled reads unambiguously at a glance: dimmed content, the cost line swapped for the blocking reason in negative color, and a tooltip naming the deficit. No opacity-mush over borders.

Stat rows — the ledger reads like a ledger (tabular numerals, hairline rows, no chips)
Wood24±0
Stone18+8
Gold12+4
Food14−2
Influence1+2
Happiness−0.5−0.5
Resource spine — full-width band replaces six bordered pills
24±0 18+8 12+4 14−2 1+2 −0.5−0.5
Vertical tab rail — the roadmap's ledger, room for every future tab
Cities
Build
Pops
Market
Victory
Codex

Active tab = clay notch + tinted icon. No pills, no background swaps. The rail absorbs Compendium now, Assembly in Phase 3, Trade in Phase 4 — the horizontal five-up row it replaces was already full.

Tooltip — black-figure inverse, so it reads as "held above the table" in both themes
Upgrade — blocked
Needs 30 stone — you hold 18. The quarry landmark north of Miltos's colony yields +6 if settled.
VIII

Layout Anatomy

The shared skeleton every direction agrees on — and the one your own todo already sketched: verbs leave the right rail for a bottom bar, the ledger gets vertical tabs, the map gains width. Shown at both design sizes.

Zone@1440×900@1280×800Notes
Top band52px48pxSeason medallion + resource spine + event slivers + turn banner + roster. Replaces today's tall two-row topbar and the bottom pill band.
Ledger rail52px, always visible48pxVertical icon tabs (your filed todo) — scales past 8 tabs
Ledger panel~300px, docked~280pxAn economy game lives in its ledger — zero-click stays. Collapses to the rail for a cinematic map when you want it.
Map≈870×744 docked · ~1330 collapsed≈750×640 dockedvs ~740×745 today at 1440 and ~610px at 1280 — the map gains at both sizes even fully docked
Verb bar64px, vase-black rail60pxVerbs in play order on a dark band (the one dark anchor — figure-ground makes legality readable) + deck counters + END TURN seal anchored right
Chronicle~180–260px docked feedcollapsible to railJudges struck the "ticker" idea: hiding an always-visible log is a regression in a log-heavy economy game

No page scroll at either size; only ledger content and chronicle scroll internally. Z-model: map 0 · tile overlays 10 · chrome 100 · floats 110 · tooltips 200 · modal 300 · toasts 400 — fixes today's inversion where modal backdrops (z-20) render under the topbar (z-60). Elevation = exactly two shadows (float, ceremony); persistent chrome casts none.

IX

Modal Grammar — the Ceremony Dial

One scaffold (scrim → centered sheet → title → body → action row), one dial that steps ornament up with the stakes. This replaces all three current grammars, and its shell is refactor R1 in the engineering report — build the shell once, theme it once, fix Escape once.

Dial 0 · Picker

Routine acts

420px sheet on the chrome surface. Grow, Move, Build target, confirmations. Type + hairlines only, ghost-cancel + one primary.

Escape ✓ · backdrop ✓ · Enter confirms
Dial 1 · Tablet

Acts with weight

560px tablet: Calm, Venture stake, bank detail, the d6/d20 tables, the compendium. Engraved seal above the title; one keyline — the single ornament allowed outside cards.

Escape ✓ unless a roll is pending
Dial 2 · Card

The game speaks

Event reveals. The existing ornate parchment card, presented on a minimal matte — the only place engraved frames and full art live.

Blocking cards hide close and ignore Escape until resolved
Dial 3 · Rite

Era moments

Game over, era transitions. Full-bleed black-figure tableau, laurel engraving, per-seat ledger of the age.

No close — only "New Game / Review the Board"

Dice tables render as ruled ledger rows — die numeral (tabular, in an engraved die glyph) · rule · outcome text; d20 splits into two columns of ten. On a roll the numeral flips three times over ~400ms, settles, the struck row takes a clay tick + wash while the rest drop to 55%. The number is the drama — no 3D dice. The same table component serves the compendium read-only ("everything rollable is viewable before it happens").

X

The Chrome Question — Three Directions, One Verdict

Everything above is consensus: three independently-developed art directions converged on the same palette collapse, icon language, board grammar, modal dial, and layout skeleton. They divide on exactly one thing — what the persistent chrome is made of. Three specialist judges (playtest readability · brand craft · shipping feasibility) scored all three adversarially.

Recommended base

Katalogos — the Catalogue of Ships

A fine-press exhibition catalog laid open beside the artifact: one paper ground, hierarchy from type, hairlines, and air. All ceremony lives in the board and the cards.

lean-abstract · quiet-high ceremony · airy

  • "There is no panel" — de-boxing by deletion; the cheapest possible compliance with Law Γ
  • Only direction where every migration step ships: shell stays parchment, topology stays docked
  • Strictest craft: 9 type roles with CI enforcement, status-vs-owner pixel-class rule
  • Weakness the grafts fix: glance-poor board and text-only verbs — museum curation, bad playtesting
78craft61playtest82shipping221total
Board grammar adopted

The Painted Table

The screen is one physical table: the sea chart full-bleed, chrome as flat material objects laid on it — ceramic tablet, ivory margin, vase-black lacquer rail.

direct · matte-tactile · dense

  • Won the playtest lens outright: docked ledger, biggest readable board, class-coded pop beads
  • Most ownable concept — nobody's 4X looks like a photographed tabletop with sea-grout coastlines
  • Fatal as a whole: coupled migration steps (ugly intermediate states), three-material chrome risks "three apps taped together", and the vase-white seat shipped broken
71craft78playtest67shipping216total
Steals only

Strategos — the Black-Figure Command Deck

Full-bleed map framed by slim vase-black chrome: the board is the sunlit clay, the interface is the black glaze — a dark AAA HUD speaking Kerameikos.

emblematic · low chrome ceremony · high density

  • Best one-sentence thesis and the biggest map of the three
  • Judged the rewrite-disguised-as-a-theme: chrome polarity inversion means no shippable intermediate state, plus blur-over-pannable-SVG paint cost
  • Exiles the parchment equity to modals — contradicts your recorded "flat AAA on the parchment theme"
  • Its amphora thesis survives where it belongs: the ceremony register (tooltips, game-over tableau, the verb rail)
63craft68playtest52shipping183total

Verdict — Katalogos constitution, with a mandatory graft package

Katalogos wins two lenses and is the only direction a solo dev can migrate to while the game keeps evolving. But the playtest judge's critique is fatal if ignored: it satisfies "quiet board" by removing information. So the recommendation is Katalogos as the chrome constitution, amended by the panel's own steal list:

  1. The vase-black verb rail (from Painted Table / Strategos). The bottom verb bar becomes the one dark band on the parchment shell — verbs gain figure-ground, disabled-at-32% reads unambiguously, END TURN anchors it as a clay seal. This single graft fixes Katalogos's text-only-verb discoverability gamble and gives the screen its black-figure accent without inverting the chrome.
  2. Painted Table's board-token grammar: class-coded pop beads (solid = citizen, ring = freeman, dark = slave — the social ladder stays visible on the board), sea-grout gaps as the coastline, and full-opacity thin owner rims (not 22% whispers). Katalogos's own slots-appear-on-targeting and illegal-tiles-dim-to-80% stay.
  3. The black-figure tooltip (Painted Table): vase-black ground, ivory text, ochre numerals — floating layers instantly read as "held above the table" (rendered live in section VII).
  4. Strategos's engineering steals: the token-migration receipts table as a required deliverable before touching base.css; the tnum-survival-through-subsetting smoke test; the two-shadow elevation policy and tokened z-model; -lit status variants for dark surfaces; the dead-center turn banner; the 150ms beat between die settle and row highlight.
  5. Readability amendments (playtest lens + owner, 2026-07-13): the polychrome board — four kind-glaze fills + the bone yield chit (owner ruling; supersedes the neutral ramp both this verdict and v0.1 originally carried); slot sockets visible at rest on every tile, settled or not; pops as class glyph + count numeral, never one mark per pop; owner rims always keylined ink+ivory; the 11px label is exempt from the 1280 rem step; event slivers keep one effect line at 1280; legal targets are always aegean, never ochre; iconography ratified stylized (A/B tested 2026-07-13) under §V's legibility law.

What this buys: your recorded preferences honored simultaneously (flat AAA, parchment theme, no boxes), the event cards kept as the undisputed ceremony peak, a board that finally out-reads the chrome — and a migration where every single step ships.

XI

Applying It — the Order of Work

Sequenced with the engineering report so the reskin lands on consolidated components instead of nine copies. Every phase leaves the game shippable.

Phase A
≈ a weekend

Consolidate before you paint. Refactors R1–R3: one ModalShell (ports all nine dialogs, fixes Escape + z-inversion), one ResourceChips, one data-driven CommandVerb. Purity Stage 0–1 alongside (controller eviction, path aliases, assets → public/).

Phase B
S — days

Token constitution. Move tokens to :root; collapse tans/inks per section III; add the missing families (card ramp, status, sea, shadows ×2); re-point the ~200 alpha literals via RGB-triplet derivation; swap player colors to the six glazes; self-host Cinzel + Alegreya and sweep the 50 font combos into the 8 roles.

Phase C
M — the visible one

De-box the chrome, re-zone the shell. Delete panel frames/corner marks — hierarchy becomes type + hairlines + air on the one paper ground; land the vase-black verb rail, vertical ledger rail, and top-band resource spine per section VIII (this is your filed ledger/verb-bar todo, executed); chronicle stays a docked, collapsible feed.

Phase D
M–L

The board becomes pieces. HexMap split (R6), then: ceramic ramp, emblem-whisper, grout gaps, settlement discs with blazons, pop beads, recessed sockets, owner rims, zoom LODs.

Phase E
M — parallelizable

The kiln batch. ~34 glyphs in one style-locked Nano Banana session (section V table), mask derivation, atlas splitting, asset cleanup. Can run alongside C/D since the mask plumbing doesn't change.

Phase F
S

Motion + polish. Four duration tokens, resource-delta ticks, turn-handoff sweep, dice settle; prefers-reduced-motion collapse. Then playtest the brandbook itself: player-glaze legibility on the real board, Cinzel at 11px, disabled-verb readability.

Open questions — decisions this book deliberately leaves to you

  • Ratify (or veto) the section X verdict. If the Painted Table's tabletop materiality pulls at you more than the catalog's restraint, the core system is identical — the fork only affects Phase C.
  • Player glazes on the real board: Melichron's contrast on ceramic ground, and the Porphyra/Glaukos back seats, need one honest colorblind pass in playtest; the blazon rule makes any tuning safe.
  • Marcellus vs Cinzel for display: Marcellus is single-weight and softer, ~60% smaller font bill; Cinzel has weights and a harder chisel. This book uses Cinzel; both are OFL and swappable in one token.
  • How much warmth creeps back: the Painted Table's thickness-edge and tablet chamfers are available later as a "materiality" seasoning on the Katalogos base if playtests find it austere — one token, not a re-theme.

Hegemony Brandbook v0.1 — a working draft to expand and apply, not a museum piece.

Grounded in: six-agent code/asset/doc audit · live-app captures at 1440×900 and 1280×800 · three adversarially-judged art directions · the Kerameikos token layer already in src/styles/base.css · docs/resource-color-rollback-note.md · your filed ledger & verb-bar todo.

Companion: Frontend Architecture Report (code health · multiplayer readiness · monorepo staging).
